Elder Spiritist's Greaves  
 

Lore Item
Slot: LEGS
Charges: Unlimited
AC: 10
STR: +6 WIS: +6 MANA: +55
Effect: Skin like Diamond (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 12.0) at Level 45
WT: 7.5 Size: LARGE
Class: DRU
Race: HUM ELF HEF HFL DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Slot: LEGS
Charges: Unlimited

STR: +6 WIS: +6 MANA: +55

WT: 7.5 Size: LARGE

Rarity: Common

Elder Spirit's Greaves
QuoteReply
# Oct 31 2003 at 12:12 PM Rating: Decent
jayah
4 posts
Does anybody know what lvl you have to be to use the effect?????? Just bought a pair for my lvl 14 druid. Can't use the effect yet. Seems a waste if you cant use them until 39 when you get the spell anyways!!!!!!!!
▲ Page top
RE: Elder Spirit's Greaves
QuoteReply
# Dec 12 2003 at 8:22 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591781101573
View User Forum
Scholar
161 posts
All elder spiritist armor (as well as any class specific kunark armor can be used for effects at lvl 46)
▲ Page top
RE: Elder Spirit's Greaves
QuoteReply
# Jun 30 2004 at 9:29 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592760228035
287 posts
Kunark is lvl 45 i thought...
▲ Page top
RE: Elder Spirit's Greaves
QuoteReply
# Dec 12 2003 at 8:19 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592257637403
Scholar
120 posts
These are usable at 50, and they are not a waste at all. SLD takes some serious mana to cast, and this is a manaless cast. And not bad stats for legs either.
▲ Page top
price
QuoteReply
# May 14 2003 at 1:17 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591773086302
21 posts
picked up a set of these today at the bazaar on luclin server for 8k pretty cheap i guess, even tho i got better wis on my pants now ill just keep these in my tinker's bag and use it to heal when in between kites.

Holloman Bites
39th Season Druid
Luclin server

#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 29 2002 at 9:45 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) this thing is GOD
#REDACTED, Posted: Jul 24 2002 at 9:15 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) im looking to buy these legs on quellious server i will pay 15kpp PST to dexxeaa
For the love of god
QuoteReply
# Dec 02 2001 at 5:19 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591771583984
1 post
This is what irks me so bad... NO mob in the game should be so hard that it takes half teh players on the server that night to drop it. Come on Verant... challenging is one thing, impossible is another. Let us have some fun.
▲ Page top
RE: For the love of god
QuoteReply
# Dec 11 2001 at 10:44 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591763407264
1 post
I really hope that you are not referring to Venril Sathir. As far as mobs go, he's pretty damn simple. Watched a guild take him down tonight with 16 people, and most of that was overkill. VS can be done easily with only two skillful groups.
▲ Page top
RE: For the love of god
QuoteReply
# Dec 27 2001 at 5:17 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593965735719
17 posts
Went on my first VS raid on Christmas Eve. We had about 30 people and that was definitely overkill. Only one person died and he was only lvl 49. Give the main tank time enough to get all the agro with clerics divining and chanters runing and he will drop in no time.
▲ Page top
RE: For the love of god
QuoteReply
# Dec 12 2001 at 8:39 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591769934300
32 posts

DW Helm
QuoteReply
# Jul 10 2001 at 10:47 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593965784038
View User Forum
14 posts
Take it like the Paladin DW Helm, mana free heal. Addmit it we dr00ds get lazy sometimes and don't always cast a regen spell like we should. So considering you can just click yourself to full health it is helpful.

My 2cp
▲ Page top
ES Legs
QuoteReply
# Jun 10 2001 at 7:07 AM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Actually, once you get your pet spell, these come in handy for buffing him up mana free. Grant it, Natureskin is much better, but for the mobs I use my pet on (greens for faction adjustment), mana free SLD is just the ticket.

I also use it to buff newbies in AC when I am hanging out auctioning there. That along with my NW boots free SoW and I am a buffing fool =)
▲ Page top
If used with a manastone...?
QuoteReply
# May 15 2001 at 5:23 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591754966424
Sage
119 posts
The stats aren't too impressive, especially with things like Kromrif Military Leggings that are much easier to get (AC 14, str+6 wis+5 sv disease+5 sv cold+5, if I remember right--obtained solo as a 49 druid with a few hours of effort).

However, and this is just speculation as I don't have the greaves myself, but...
What if the effect is combined with a manastone? It seems to me a right-clickable Skin like Diamond would be a free 200 point heal with a 12 second cast time--double the 6 second cast time of the original spell, right? With 3 uses of the manastone 180 of those hp would be converted to 60 mana. 60 mana in 12 seconds, or 30 mana a tick. Not bad, especially if you can sit during the cast time to get the 20 mana per tick or so from meditating too.
Obviously this won't work in the planes or Kunark/Velious, but it could be very useful in a few places like the hole...

Anyone tried this?

(Edit: Well, you can no longer meditate while using right clickable items. Turns out you weren't supposed to be able to in the first place.)
▲ Page top
RE: If used with a manastone...?
QuoteReply
# Jan 14 2003 at 5:23 PM Rating: Decent
dchadwick
View User Forum
Scholar
72 posts
just a little correction to your post it is now possable to meditate while casting. while sitting on a horse. best new item in the game is the horse for the luminescent staff and these pants. =0)
▲ Page top
RE: If used with a manastone...?
QuoteReply
# Dec 14 2001 at 12:53 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593375431601
42 posts
highly effective for a druid: port to old world 'meditate', and gate back to camp.

someone said lvl 60 druid could FM in 2 min using this combo...
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: May 15 2001 at 3:00 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Ugh, these are nice legs, but by the time you can get them, you already have better. Why don't they put these on stuff we can take out, like in LGuk, or Mistmoore, or similar zone where the looter can USE the item... As is, this is little more than a twink item for HLs to give to their newbie druids, like all the other ES stuff.
